metadata.title: RaceDash — Your lap times through a new lens
metadata.description: RaceDash turns your track footage into broadcast-grade race videos — with live timing, telemetry and lap-by-lap overlays baked right in.

hero.headline: Your lap times through a new lens.
hero.subhead: RaceDash turns your track footage into broadcast-grade race videos — with live timing, telemetry and lap-by-lap overlays baked right in.

trust.line: Built with racers across karting, club and endurance paddocks.

primitives.heading: Three steps. No editing suite required.

items.heading: Bring in your session.
items.body: Drop in your footage and bring in your lap times — from your timekeeper, your on-dash logger, or typed in by hand. RaceDash takes it from there.

items.heading: Jump straight to your fastest lap.
items.body: RaceDash lines up every lap to every frame and flags your quickest one. Watch it back, learn from it, and be on the grid before the next session is called.

items.heading: Broadcast-grade videos, baked in.
items.body: Pick an overlay style and let RaceDash compose the final video. Render on your machine, or offload it to the cloud.

showcase.heading: Lap times belong on the video, not on a spreadsheet.
showcase.body: RaceDash writes every lap, every position and your session's fastest time straight onto the frame. No cross-referencing a timing sheet with a video player — the context is baked in, so you can just watch the lap and learn.

items.heading: Timekeeper integrations
items.body: Pull sessions straight from Alpha, SpeedHive/MyLaps, Daytona and TeamSport. No typing lap times by hand.

items.heading: Works with your logger
items.body: MyChron, Alfano or RaceBox — RaceDash reads your session straight off the SD card.

items.heading: Fastest lap, in one click
items.body: RaceDash flags your best lap of the session so you can jump straight to it between runs.

items.heading: Multiple overlay styles
items.body: Banner, Geometric, Esports, Minimal, Modern — and more on the way. Pick one and you're ready to render.

items.heading: Lap-by-lap renders
items.body: Export your fastest lap, your whole session, or any subset — pick the laps that mattered.

items.heading: Cloud rendering
items.body: Offload the heavy frames to RaceDash Cloud and keep your laptop cool.

why.heading: Built by racers who got tired of being video editors.
why.body: RaceDash started at Club100. Between heats, we'd want to watch our fastest lap and work out where we'd left time on the table — but finding it meant scrubbing through footage with a paper timing sheet in hand, and Premiere Pro was no help. So we built the tool we needed: one that knows what a lap is, knows which one was your fastest, and has your video ready before you're back on grid.

pricing.heading: One license. Every track day.

plus.name: Plus
plus.tagline: For the driver with a camera and a timing sheet.

pro.name: Pro
pro.tagline: For the racer who doesn't have time to wait on a render.

pricing.credits: Cloud render credits sold separately · from £7 for 50 RC

waitlistCta.heading: Be first on the grid.
waitlistCta.body: Drop your email. We'll let you know the moment RaceDash is ready.
waitlistCta.success: You're on the list. We'll be in touch.
waitlistCta.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

footer.copyright: © 2026 RaceDash · Made by racers, for racers.

metadata.title: Pricing — RaceDash
metadata.description: Plus and Pro subscriptions, cloud render credits, and everything in between. Simple pricing for racers.

pricingPage.heading: One license. Every track day.
pricingPage.subhead: RaceDash is a yearly subscription. Pick the tier that fits how you race, top up cloud render credits when you need them, and cancel any time.
pricingPage.creditsBody: Cloud renders are metered in credits. Top up when you need to. Credits never expire unless your subscription lapses.

faq.a: One credit renders one minute of standard 1080p footage. 4K and higher framerates cost more credits per minute — the exact multiplier is shown in the app before you hit render.

Yes. Your subscription stays active until the end of the billing period you've already paid for. After that, your desktop app stops activating new renders but your existing exports stay yours forever.

faq.q: Do I need Pro to use RaceDash?
faq.a: No. Plus gets you the full desktop app, timekeeper integrations, and one cloud render slot. Pro adds data logger support, more cloud render slots, and 250 GB of cloud storage.

faq.q: What happens to my footage if I don't pay for cloud rendering?
faq.a: Nothing. RaceDash renders locally on your machine by default. Cloud rendering is an opt-in convenience — your video never leaves your laptop unless you ask it to.
Not at launch. We're running a waitlist instead — early signups get a discount on their first year.

metadata.title: About — RaceDash
metadata.description: The Club100 origin story behind RaceDash.

about.heading: Racers first. Software engineers second.
about.body: RaceDash is built by people who race. We know the cadence of a race weekend, the frustration of watching a lap back on a phone screen in the paddock, and the small joy of finally finding the corner you left time on.
storyParagraphs: RaceDash started at Club100 in Buckmore Park. Between heats we'd sit in the paddock with a paper timing sheet and a phone, trying to find the lap we wanted to watch. We knew which lap was our fastest — the timing sheet said so — but the footage on our phones was 45 minutes of continuous recording with no markers, no context, and no way to jump to the one corner that mattered.

We tried Premiere Pro. It worked, just about, but by the time we'd finished exporting we'd already driven home. The whole point of watching the lap was to learn from it before the next session, and that window had closed.

So we built RaceDash. It knows what a lap is. It knows which one was your fastest. It pulls timing from whoever's running the event — Alpha, MyLaps, Daytona, TeamSport — or straight off the SD card in your MyChron. And it gets your video ready in the time it takes to grab a drink.
We're still racing. This is still the tool we want to use. That's the whole brief.

values.heading: Your footage stays yours
values.body: Everything renders locally by default. Cloud rendering is opt-in, and we don't keep your uploads longer than we have to.

values.heading: Built for the paddock
values.body: RaceDash has to work on a laptop with patchy Wi-Fi, between sessions, on five hours' sleep. That's the real environment — so that's how we build it.

values.heading: No unnecessary complexity
values.body: We don't add features because our competitors do. We add them because a racer told us they'd stopped watching their footage.

metadata.title: Contact — RaceDash
metadata.description: Get in touch with the RaceDash team.

contact.heading: Get in touch.
contact.body: We're a small team, still in pre-launch, and we read every message. Drop us a note and we'll get back to you.
contact.comingSoonBody: Our support inbox goes live when the app launches. Until then, the fastest way to reach us is the waitlist form — tell us what you'd like to talk about in the email you use to sign up and we'll follow up personally.

changelog.heading: Built in public.
changelog.body: Every release, every fix, every small improvement — logged here. Starts on launch day.
changelog.emptyBody: The first release is in the oven. Join the waitlist to be the first to know when it's out.
